name: rating-group
description: Star rating input with accessible radio group pattern

meta.name: RatingGroup
meta.description: Star rating input with accessible radio group pattern

props.name: onValueChange
props.description: Called with the new value when it changes.

props.name: disabled
props.description: When true, disables the control and removes it from the tab order.

props.name: readOnly
props.description: When true, the value is shown but cannot be edited.

props.name: labels
props.description: Overrides for the component’s user-visible strings (i18n).

whenToUse: Capturing a discrete subjective rating on a small fixed scale (e.g. 1–5 stars)
Displaying an existing rating read-only as a compact star summary
whenNotToUse: Choosing along a continuous or large numeric range — use Slider
Entering an exact bounded number with steppers — use NumberInput
A non-rating single choice among labeled options — use Radio or SegmentedControl

antiPatterns.bad: <Slider min={1} max={5} step={1} /> labeled as a star rating
antiPatterns.why: A slider communicates a continuous magnitude; RatingGroup uses radio semantics so each star is an exclusive discrete choice announced as "N of M stars"

related.name: Slider
related.reason: Use for continuous or larger numeric ranges

related.name: NumberInput
related.reason: Use when an exact typed number is needed rather than a rating

intent.a11yRationale: Each star is a <button role="radio"> inside a role="radiogroup" with aria-checked on the selected value and an aria-label like "3 of 5 stars"; when readOnly or disabled the stars drop out of the tab order so a non-interactive rating is not announced as actionable.

flexibility.note: Star styling must resolve to the listed --cascivo-* tokens
max sets the scale and labels.rating customizes the per-star announcement
